rulings.Case Title: PETER CHILDRESS VS. WILLIAM MURPHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages And Attorneys Fess

rulings.Case Title: OBBIA FORTUNE VS. MARKET HALL FOODS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 21, 2010, line 7. DEFENDANT MARKET HALL FOODS, SARA WILSON AND TONY WILSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST. AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 2nd. cause of action (NIED). Plaintiff must plead each element of negligence with particularity, including what duty was owed and how it was breeched. Overruled as to 3rd. cause of action (IIED). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INPRO II LICENSING SARL, A LUXEMBOURG LIMITED VS. LITE-ON TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Case On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum [Ccp 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 21, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANT LITE-ON TECHNOLOGY CORPORATION'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Case On Ground Of Inconvenient Forum [Ccp 418.10(A)(2), 410.30(A)]. GRANTED. This case is stayed pending disposition of the Taiwanese action. California has little interest in this case which involves foreing companies. The evidence shows there are numerous witnesses and almost all of them are out of State. Taiwan is a suitable alternative forum. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal 3rd. 744. =(302/CWW)
